parameterGroups.label: Gate
parameterGroups.rationale: Noise gate stage - attenuates signal below a threshold. Enable toggle gates the entire stage.

parameters.name: GateEnabled
parameters.description: Enables the noise gate to attenuate signals below the threshold

parameters.name: GateThreshold
parameters.description: The level threshold in dB below which the gate attenuates the signal

parameters.name: GateAttack
parameters.description: Attack time in milliseconds for the gate to open when the signal exceeds the threshold

parameters.name: GateRelease
parameters.description: Release time in milliseconds for the gate to close when the signal falls below the threshold

parameters.name: GateReduction
parameters.description: A read-only slot for reading out the gate gain reduction

parameterGroups.label: Compressor
parameterGroups.rationale: Compressor stage - reduces dynamic range above a threshold. Enable toggle gates the entire stage.

parameters.name: CompressorEnabled
parameters.description: Enables the compressor to reduce dynamic range of signals above the threshold

parameters.name: CompressorThreshold
parameters.description: The level threshold in dB above which compression begins

parameters.name: CompressorRatio
parameters.description: Compression ratio where higher values apply more aggressive gain reduction

parameters.name: CompressorAttack
parameters.description: Attack time in milliseconds for the compressor to respond to signals above the threshold

parameters.name: CompressorRelease
parameters.description: Release time in milliseconds for the compressor to return to normal gain after compression

parameters.name: CompressorReduction
parameters.description: A read-only slot for reading out the compressor gain reduction

parameters.name: CompressorMakeup
parameters.description: Automatically applies makeup gain to compensate for compression gain reduction

parameterGroups.label: Limiter
parameterGroups.rationale: Limiter stage - prevents signal from exceeding a ceiling. Enable toggle gates the entire stage.

parameters.name: LimiterEnabled
parameters.description: Enables the limiter to prevent signals from exceeding the threshold

parameters.name: LimiterThreshold
parameters.description: The ceiling level in dB that the limiter prevents the signal from exceeding

parameters.name: LimiterAttack
parameters.description: Attack time in milliseconds for the limiter to respond to signals above the threshold

parameters.name: LimiterRelease
parameters.description: Release time in milliseconds for the limiter to return to normal gain after limiting

parameters.name: LimiterReduction
parameters.description: A read-only slot for reading out the limiter gain reduction

parameters.name: LimiterMakeup
parameters.description: Automatically applies makeup gain to compensate for limiter gain reduction

gaps.question: What is the processing order of the three stages (gate, compressor, limiter) in applyEffect()? Are they always in this fixed order, or can they be reordered?
gaps.context: Need to confirm the serial chain order for the pseudo-code and to document stage interaction.

gaps.question: When a stage is disabled, is it fully bypassed (zero CPU) or does it still process silently? Do the three stages operate independently on the same buffer in series?
gaps.context: Users need to know whether disabled stages consume CPU and whether stages interact.

gaps.question: How are the compressor and limiter makeup gain values calculated? Is it based on threshold and ratio, or on measured gain reduction?
gaps.context: Users enabling makeup gain need to understand what compensation is applied.

gaps.question: How are GateReduction, CompressorReduction, and LimiterReduction updated? Per-sample peak tracking with decay? What is the decay rate?
gaps.context: These read-only parameters are used for metering display - need to document their behaviour.

gaps.question: What is the limiterPending flag for? Is there a crossfade when the limiter is enabled/disabled?
gaps.context: The limiter enable toggle may have special transition behaviour to avoid clicks.

gaps.question: Are all three stages per-sample processors? What is the approximate CPU cost of each stage?
gaps.context: Need to assess CPU profile and document whether this is a lightweight or heavyweight effect.

gaps.question: The CompressorRatio range is 1-32 but the code passes 1/ratio to the compressor. Is the user-facing value the standard ratio (e.g. 4:1) or the inverse?
gaps.context: Need to confirm the parameter table describes the ratio correctly from the user perspective.

notes: Dynamics has no modulation chains at all - purely parameter-driven. The three stages (gate, compressor, limiter) each have enable toggles suggesting conditional processing paths. The Reduction parameters are read-only metering outputs. CompressorMakeup and LimiterMakeup are auto-calculated compensation toggles, not gain sliders. All stages default to disabled (0.0), and all thresholds default to 0 dB.
